title: SmarterP Connector — ERP semantic layer
description: Maps business language to views, columns, and T-SQL date filters. Edit this file when KPI definitions change; NLQ script loads it via NLQ_SEMANTIC_FILE.

catalog_meta.note: This file indexes all 28 views with purpose and KPI columns only — not every column. Use schema_catalog.txt for column-level detail.

time_periods.today: CAST([XnDt] AS DATE) = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
time_periods.yesterday: CAST([XnDt] AS DATE) = CAST(DATEADD(DAY, -1, GETDATE()) AS DATE)
time_periods.this_week: [XnDt] >= DATEADD(DAY, -(DATEPART(dw, GETDATE()) - 1), CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY, 1, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
time_periods.mtd: [XnDt] >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()), MONTH(GETDATE()), 1) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY, 1, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
time_periods.qtd: [XnDt] >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()), ((MONTH(GETDATE()) - 1) / 3) * 3 + 1, 1) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY, 1, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
time_periods.ytd: [XnDt] >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()), 1, 1) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY, 1, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
time_periods.last_7d: [XnDt] >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY, 1, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
time_periods.last_30d: [XnDt] >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY, 1, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))
time_periods.last_6m: [XnDt] >= DATEADD(MONTH, -6, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY, 1, CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE))

branch.note: Use on SLSXNS, APP_REPORT, SLS_REPORT, STOCK_REPORT

category.note: Short code for filters; full [Category] for display labels

department.note: Do not assume = 'Women'; use LIKE unless user gives exact code

salesperson.note: Use salesperson_lines view with CashmemoDt — not on SLSXNS

hour.note: SLSXNS.[XnDt] is DATE only — hourly peak analysis is NOT supported on this view

sales_app.purpose: Primary aggregated sales — dashboard default; 90% of analytics

sales_memo.purpose: Memo-level sales with product attributes; margin via NetSlsCostValue
sales_memo.note: Use XnMemoDate for period filters — not XnDt

ai_sales_data.purpose: Lightweight AI sales summary for fast trends and NLQ

ai_branch.purpose: Branch master — names, geography, address (no sales amounts)

ai_customer.purpose: Customer master — new customer KPI, contact, credit, branch

ai_stock.purpose: Simple stock by ItemId and BranchId — Products page

salesperson_lines.purpose: Top salespersons chart — has SalesPersonName and CashmemoDt
salesperson_lines.note: Preferred for salesperson ranking — not SLSXNS

sales_apr.purpose: Application (APR) sales — similar grain to APP_REPORT

branch_list.purpose: Branch dimension for filters, dropdowns, branch intelligence

product_master.purpose: Item master — style, fabric, supplier, MRP (not transactional)

purchase_returns.purpose: Purchase return transactions by branch and supplier

pur_report.purpose: Purchase receipts by branch, supplier, category, item

purchases.purpose: Detailed purchase and return transactions with tax breakdown

sales_billcount.purpose: Daily bill count per branch — footfall / transaction volume

sales_lines.purpose: Canonical net sales lines — MTD/today revenue, returns, tax (preferred for revenue KPIs)
sales_lines.note: XnDt is DATE only — no hourly analysis

stock.purpose: Current stock on hand by branch, supplier, category, item

supplier_pur.purpose: Supplier-wise purchase history with product attributes

vendor_master.purpose: Vendor/party master — contact, tax, credit, MSME (no transactions)

ai_supplier.purpose: Supplier dimension lookup for procurement NLQ

mst_items.purpose: Complete item master — hierarchy, parameters, supplier, pricing

guardrails: Default revenue: SUM([NetSlsNetAmount]) on dbo.VW_MB_POWERBI_SLSXNS_REPORT (sales_lines) with [XnDt] date filter.
Dashboard-style aggregates: VW_MB_POWERBI_APP_REPORT (sales_app) — may differ from SLSXNS daily totals.
Footfall / bill count: VW_MB_POWERBI_SLS_BILLCOUNT (CashmemoDt, BillCount) or BillCount on APP_REPORT.
Salesperson top-N: VW_MB_POWERBI_SLS_DATA_WITHOUT_ITEMID — never SalesPersonName on SLSXNS.
Stock on hand: VW_MB_POWERBI_STOCK_REPORT or VwAIStockData; GIT stock: VW_MB_POWERBI_CBS_WITH_GIT.
STOCK_REPORT keys items by ItemId (not Itemcode) — join VW_MB_POWERBI_PRODUCT_MASTER on ItemId to match APP_REPORT Itemcode.
Transfers: STI_REPORT (inbound) / STO_REPORT (outbound) — use SourceBranchAlias and TargetBranchAlias.
Masters (no amounts): branch_list, category_master, vendor_master, product_master, VwMstItems — join to transactional views.
Never hard-code calendar dates for rolling MTD/today; use GETDATE() expressions.
[XnDt] on SLSXNS is DATE type: DATEPART(HOUR, [XnDt]) fails or returns hour 0 only — refuse hourly peak queries or state limitation.
Long history (24+ months) on line-level sales views: aggregate to month in one pass.
Women's / department filters: use LIKE on [DepartmentShortName], not exact = 'Women'.
Full column list (~658 cols) is in schema_catalog.txt — semantic file lists KPI columns per view only.

example_nlp_sql.question: Compare Chennai vs Bangalore sales this month

questions: Compare by quantity instead of revenue
Compare Chennai vs Bangalore sales this month

few_shot: Q: Total net sales today -> sales_lines (SLSXNS), CAST([XnDt] AS date) = CAST(GETDATE() AS date), SUM([NetSlsNetAmount]).
Q: MTD revenue by branch -> sales_lines, MTD on [XnDt], GROUP BY [BranchAlias], ORDER BY revenue DESC.
Q: Top category MTD -> sales_lines, GROUP BY [Category] or [CategoryShortName], TOP N ORDER BY SUM([NetSlsNetAmount]) DESC.
Q: Top salesperson MTD -> salesperson_lines, filter CashmemoDt MTD, GROUP BY [SalesPersonName], SUM([SalesNetAmount]).
Q: Bills today by branch -> sales_billcount, CAST([CashmemoDt] AS date) = today, SUM([BillCount]) GROUP BY [BranchId].
Q: Month-wise since Apr 2024 -> sales_lines, monthly GROUP BY DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR([XnDt]),MONTH([XnDt]),1), one scan.
Q: Stock by branch -> stock (STOCK_REPORT), SUM([StockQty]), GROUP BY [BranchAlias].
Q: Net purchases MTD -> purchases (PURXNS), PurInvDate MTD, SUM([NetPurNetAmount]).
Q: Stock transfer in MTD -> stock_transfer_in, filter [XnDt], SUM([StiQty]) by [TargetBranchAlias].

Q: Supplier contribution % MTD -> USE CTE + window: WITH sup AS (SELECT [SupplierName], SUM([NetSlsNetAmount]) AS Rev FROM dbo.VW_MB_POWERBI_SLSXNS_REPORT WITH (NOLOCK) WHERE [XnDt] >= DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY,1,CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)) GROUP BY [SupplierName]) SELECT TOP 500 [SupplierName], Rev, CAST(100.0*Rev/NULLIF(SUM(Rev) OVER(),0) AS decimal(18,4)) AS ContributionPct FROM sup ORDER BY ContributionPct DESC.

Q: Monthly revenue trend last 12 months -> sales_lines, GROUP BY DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR([XnDt]),MONTH([XnDt]),1), WHERE [XnDt] >= DATEADD(MONTH,-12,DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(GETDATE()),MONTH(GETDATE()),1)) AND [XnDt] < DATEADD(DAY,1,CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)), ORDER BY MonthStart ASC. NEVER scan all history — always cap at max_history_months months back.

query_policies._comment: Enforced by terminal_openai_nlq_sql.py _apply_query_policies(). Override per-run with --no-policy.
query_policies.default_period_note: When no time period is detected in the question and the query targets a sales/revenue metric, MTD is silently applied. The user sees a yellow notice. Pass --no-policy to disable.
query_policies.max_history_note: Trend and history queries capped at 24 months back on line-level views. The prompt is annotated with a DATEADD(MONTH,-24,...) start floor so the model cannot scan all history.
query_policies.require_date_note: Guardrail injected into system prompt: never generate a query on SLSXNS without a WHERE on [XnDt] unless user explicitly requests all-time totals.
query_policies.heavy_query_message: This question may scan years of line-level data and could time out. Consider adding 'last 12 months' or 'last 24 months'. Proceeding -- use Ctrl+C to cancel.
query_policies.blocked_dimensions_message: Hourly analysis is not supported -- [XnDt] on SLSXNS is DATE only (no time component). Ask for daily trends instead.
